acceptedVia: chat 2026-07-23 — diagnosis candidate pasted and confirmed after the board was fully judged
summary: Safari's back-forward cache restores the pre-navigation DOM without firing popstate, so the URL changes while the list does not. H2 validated by exact correlation with pageshow.persisted; the event wiring, the pure filter, and the race hypothesis were each falsified by direct probes.

decisions.rationale: H2 correlates perfectly across twenty traversals; the other three died to direct probes, not intuition.

exampleWorking.conclusion: H2 is the cause: Safari bfcache restores the pre-navigation DOM with pageshow.persisted=true and no popstate, so renderFromUrl never re-runs. H1 falsified (handler fires everywhere else), H3 falsified (pure core property-tested stable), H4 falsified (synchronous render, nothing to race). Fix direction — pageshow listener vs bfcache opt-out — is deliberately out of scope here.

canonicalBody:
# Diagnosis — stale rows after back navigation

Validated: bfcache restore without popstate (exact correlation with
pageshow.persisted across twenty traversals). Falsified: missing popstate
wiring, filter instability, render race — each by a direct probe. The fix
is a separate decision; this record is the diagnosis only.
